original_hypothesis.title: LiquidateWithReplacement No Swap Fee Charged on Debt Reissuance
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When executeLiquidateWithReplacement reissues debt to the replacement borrower, it calculates `issuanceValue = futureValue * PERCENT / (PERCENT + ratePerTenor)` and sends this to the new borrower. The spread `futureValue - issuanceValue` goes to feeRecipient as `liquidatorProfitBorrowToken`. However, NO swap fee is charged on this debt reissuance. In contrast, normal debt creation via buyCreditMarket/sellCreditMarket always charges a swap fee. This means liquidateWithReplacement effectively creates new debt at a reduced cost, and the savings go to feeRecipient rather than being properly distributed.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The absence of swap fee on liquidateWithReplacement debt reissuance creates an inconsistency with normal market operations. The entire interest spread goes to feeRecipient without any swap fee deduction.

proof_of_signal.pattern: issuanceValue calculated without swap fee deduction; entire spread goes to feeRecipient without fee accounting

preconditions: A valid replacement borrower exists with a borrow offer
The loan is liquidatable (ACTIVE + underwater, or OVERDUE)

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: For 10% APR on 1-year loan, issuanceValue ≈ 90.9% of futureValue. The 9.1% spread goes to feeRecipient. Normal lending would deduct ~0.5% swap fee.
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Keepers (or feeRecipient if colluding) could prefer liquidateWithReplacement to capture full spread
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Missing swap fee = swapFeeAPR * tenor / YEAR * issuanceValue per liquidation

economic_questions: Is the missing swap fee intentional (liquidation incentive) or an oversight?
How much swap fee revenue is lost per liquidateWithReplacement vs normal lending?
validation_requirements: Compare fee structures between liquidateWithReplacement and normal buyCreditMarket
Quantify swap fee gap for representative loan parameters

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: ~0.5% of loan value in missing swap fees per event
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Small per event, scales with liquidation volume

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: Keeper needs borrowAToken to pay futureValue

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Protocol fee revenue (reduced swap fee collection)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Swap fees on all liquidateWithReplacement events

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: swapFeeAPR=0.005e18, typical remaining tenor for liquidated loans

validation_result.mechanism: Missing swap fee on debt reissuance in liquidateWithReplacement
validation_result.impact_summary: New debt created via liquidateWithReplacement does not charge a swap fee, unlike all other debt creation paths (buyCreditMarket, sellCreditMarket). The replacement borrower receives the full issuance value without swap fee deduction, creating a fee inconsistency.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis correctly identifies that executeLiquidateWithReplacement creates new debt without charging a swap fee. In every other code path that creates new debt (buyCreditMarket and sellCreditMarket), a swap fee is calculated via AccountingLibrary.getSwapFee() and deducted from the cash flowing to the borrower, with the fee going to feeRecipient. In liquidateWithReplacement, the issuanceValue is calculated as futureValue * PERCENT / (PERCENT + ratePerTenor) and the full amount goes to the new borrower, with the interest spread (futureValue - issuanceValue) going to feeRecipient. No swap fee is deducted from the issuanceValue. This means the replacement borrower receives more cash than they would through a normal market order at the same rate. The interest spread going to feeRecipient is NOT a swap fee - it's the time value of money (interest). In normal lending, the protocol charges BOTH the interest (implicit in the rate) AND a swap fee. In liquidateWithReplacement, only the interest spread is captured.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Lines 115-116 of LiquidateWithReplacement.sol show issuanceValue calculated without any swap fee deduction. Lines 123-124 show the full issuanceValue transferred to the borrower and the full interest spread to feeRecipient. Comparing with BuyCreditMarket.sol lines where getCreditAmountOut/getCashAmountIn include swap fee calculations, and the final transfers at lines deducting fees, confirms the inconsistency.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: issuanceValue is transferred to the new borrower without any swap fee deduction. In contrast, buyCreditMarket and sellCreditMarket always deduct swap fees via AccountingLibrary.getSwapFee() or getCashAmountOut/getCreditAmountIn before transferring funds. The replacement borrower effectively receives a fee-free loan.

Normal debt creation via buyCreditMarket always calculates and deducts swap fees. The borrower receives cashAmountIn - fees. This contrasts with liquidateWithReplacement where the borrower receives the full issuanceValue.

calling_sequence: 3. executeLiquidateWithReplacement first calls state.executeLiquidate() which liquidates original borrower and sends futureValue from keeper to protocol
5. issuanceValue = Math.mulDivDown(debtPositionCopy.futureValue, PERCENT, PERCENT + ratePerTenor) - NO SWAP FEE DEDUCTION
6. liquidatorProfitBorrowToken = debtPositionCopy.futureValue - issuanceValue (just the interest spread)
7. Full issuanceValue transferred to new borrower, interest spread transferred to feeRecipient
8. No call to getSwapFee() or getSwapFeePercent() anywhere in the function
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: In LiquidateWithReplacement.sol, the executeLiquidateWithReplacement function at lines 115-116 calculates issuanceValue purely from the rate without swap fee. At line 123, the full issuanceValue is sent to the borrower. Compare with BuyCreditMarket.sol where getCreditAmountOut/getCashAmountIn always include getSwapFee calls (AccountingLibrary.sol lines 162-180, 190-220) and the borrower receives cashAmountIn - fees.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: Deploy.sol shows swapFeeAPR is set to 0.005e18 (0.5%), confirming swap fees are intended to be non-zero in production.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: A keeper calls liquidateWithReplacement on a liquidatable position. The replacement borrower (who has a standing borrow offer) receives the full issuanceValue without any swap fee deduction. Compared to a normal buyCreditMarket operation at the same rate and tenor, the borrower saves approximately swapFeeAPR * tenor / YEAR * issuanceValue. For a 1-year loan at 0.5% swap fee APR, this is ~0.5% of the issuance value. The protocol (feeRecipient) receives the interest spread but not the swap fee. While the interest spread may be larger than the swap fee, the swap fee is a separate protocol revenue stream that is bypassed.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Medium severity. The replacement borrower receives a free-of-fee loan compared to normal market participants. This creates an inconsistency in fee structures: borrowers going through liquidateWithReplacement path get better terms than those using buyCreditMarket/sellCreditMarket. For a $100,000 loan with 1-year tenor, the missing swap fee is approximately $500 per event. While the protocol still receives the interest spread via feeRecipient, the swap fee revenue stream is bypassed entirely for this path.
recommended_mitigations: Calculate and deduct swap fee from issuanceValue: uint256 swapFee = state.getSwapFee(issuanceValue, tenor); then transfer issuanceValue - swapFee to borrower and swapFee separately to feeRecipient
Alternatively, document the intentional absence of swap fee in liquidateWithReplacement if this is by design
Consider whether liquidatorProfitBorrowToken should go to msg.sender (keeper) instead of feeRecipient, as the variable name suggests (separate issue)
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy protocol with swapFeeAPR = 0.005e18. 2. Create a liquidatable position. 3. Set up replacement borrower with borrow offer. 4. Call liquidateWithReplacement. 5. Verify replacement borrower receives full issuanceValue. 6. Create equivalent loan via buyCreditMarket at same rate and tenor. 7. Compare cash received by borrower in both paths - liquidateWithReplacement borrower receives ~0.5% more.
uncertainties: Whether the absence of swap fee in liquidateWithReplacement is an intentional design decision to incentivize replacement borrowers
Whether the interest spread going to feeRecipient was intended to implicitly include the swap fee
The relationship between this issue and the separate incorrect fund routing issue (liquidatorProfitBorrowToken going to feeRecipient instead of keeper)
additional_testing_needed: Compare exact amounts received by borrowers through liquidateWithReplacement vs buyCreditMarket at identical rates and tenors
Quantify total swap fee revenue loss across typical liquidation volumes
Check if protocol documentation specifies fee behavior for liquidation with replacement
